name: Maple-Walnut Crunch Corn Muffins
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Plus 3 tbsp Pure maple syrup
1/4 cup Unsalted butter, melted and cooled
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the 1/2 cup of room-temperature butter in a small bowl until fluffy.
2. Gradually add the 3/4 cup plus 3 tbsp pure maple syrup to the butter, beating until well blended. Maple butter can be prepared up to 2 days ahead; cover and refrigerate. Bring to room temperature before using.
3. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C). Line a dozen 1/2-cup muffin cups with muffin papers.
4. In a small bowl, combine the chopped walnuts and 3 tbsp of maple syrup; set aside.
5. Sift together the flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a medium bowl. Mix in the cornmeal.
6. In a separate bowl, beat the buttermilk, eggs, melted butter, and remaining 1/4 cup of maple syrup until blended.
7. Add the egg mixture to the dry ingredients, stirring just to combine. Do not overmix.
8. Divide the batter evenly among the prepared muffin cups. Spoon some of the maple-walnut topping over each muffin, dividing evenly.
9. Bake until a tester inserted into the center of a muffin comes out clean, about 20 minutes. Remove muffins from the pan and cool for 15 minutes before serving warm with maple butter.
